blocks.content: Sunday. I didn't hear from Jonathan. I didn't reach out either. We'd been quiet before — never this particular kind of quiet. But quiet.
I thought about what Mr. Cole said. The friend who took good advice as the wrong kind of thing. How it cost something even when it worked out.
I thought about Jonathan at the game — the way he played when he stopped performing and started just doing it. The way Larry Jr. watched him from the bleachers like he was studying something.
I knew he wasn't going to be my enemy. That wasn't what this was. But something had been named and now it was named and you can't un-name something.
The week started again. Monday morning, hallway, lockers. Jonathan was there before me. He saw me coming.
He gave me a nod. Not a word. Just the nod — that slow, measured one that means: I see you. We're good. Not right now. But we're good.
Sometimes that's enough. Sometimes that's the whole conversation for a while.

blocks.prompt: The nod. Two people agreeing without saying it. What does that feel like?

options.label: Relief. It doesn't have to be dramatic. They know what they are.
It's not enough yet. There's still something that needs to be said.
